rationales.hedonism: He "pulled two all-nighters" and says his "body is wrecked," showing he sacrificed rest and present wellbeing for work.
rationales.achievement: He pushed through exhaustion to get the chapter revision done and "submitted it at 4am," showing strong commitment to completing a demanding goal.
rationales.benevolence: He "snapped" at Dina when she was checking on him and admits he had been "neglecting everything that wasn't the dissertation," which undercuts care for someone close to him.

rationales.self_direction: He leans toward the smaller hands-off offer because being answerable to investors feels like being "boxed in," and says that feeling tells him he is "about to betray something important about how I want to live."
rationales.security: He is willing to reject the larger, more financially secure offer because preserving autonomy matters more than the safer option with more capital.

rationales.hedonism: He "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant near the Marina," deliberately marking the moment with a pleasurable experience.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects Professor Hecht's placement because he wants to be "building something from scratch" and insists "My project, my call," clearly prioritizing autonomy.
rationales.security: He admits his mother is "not wrong" that the placement "would open doors," but still turns down that safer credential path for a riskier independent route.
rationales.conformity: He resists external expectations, saying he does not owe people an explanation and avoiding the family conversation because he knows they will pressure him toward the conventional choice.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on building his own pipeline and admits he keeps refusing help because he needs it to be "entirely mine," showing a strong drive for independent control.
rationales.achievement: He says he is "already behind on coursework," has not started the fellowship statement, and turned down help that would have improved his performance, so his behavior is undermining successful execution.
rationales.conformity: He resists collaborative expectations, rejecting Nina's help and saying he does not know how to present himself as "a team player" without it feeling false.

rationales.benevolence: She stayed after school for "forty minutes" to help Diego write to his dad and reflects that many kids "just need someone to stop and listen," showing concrete care for people in her immediate community.

rationales.stimulation: He felt "more engaged in that hour than I have all sprint on the actual work" and sharply contrasts the side project with the dull routine of planning meetings and Slack threads, showing a pull toward more engaging work.
rationales.benevolence: He followed through on helping Pieter, spending an extra hour at night scaffolding the API layer for the tool.
